generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects raw temperature data from physical system components as part of one-way automated data flow to Digital Shadow"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance PressureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects raw pressure data from physical system components as part of one-way automated data flow to Digital Shadow"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PressureDataTransmitted
]

instance VibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects raw vibration data from physical system components as part of one-way automated data flow to Digital Shadow"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VibrationDataTransmitted
]

instance PerformanceIndicatorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects derived performance metrics from physical system components as part of one-way automated data flow to Digital Shadow"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PerformanceIndicatorDataTransmitted
]

instance OperationalStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects operational state information from physical system components as part of one-way automated data flow to Digital Shadow"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalStateDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw temperature data collected via automated one-way flow from TemperatureSensor, transmitted using industry-standard protocols like OPC UA or MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance PressureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw pressure data collected via automated one-way flow from PressureSensor, transmitted using industry-standard protocols like OPC UA or MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PressureSensor
]

instance VibrationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw vibration data collected via automated one-way flow from VibrationSensor, transmitted using industry-standard protocols like OPC UA or MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VibrationSensor
]

instance PerformanceIndicatorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Derived performance metrics collected via automated one-way flow from PerformanceIndicatorSensor, transmitted using industry-standard protocols like OPC UA or MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PerformanceIndicatorSensor
]

instance OperationalStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Operational state information collected via automated one-way flow from OperationalStateSensor, transmitted using industry-standard protocols like OPC UA or MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalStateSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance RawSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated raw sensor data (temperature, pressure, vibration) after initial preprocessing steps including filtering and normalization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, PressureDataTransmitted, VibrationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance DerivedMetricsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived metrics including performance indicators and operational states after abstraction from raw sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PerformanceIndicatorDataTransmitted, OperationalStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance SysMLFunctionalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based functional architecture model describing system requirements, functions, and logical architecture using Internal Block Diagrams"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton model for behavioral description using state machines and component-based architecture"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcEnabler
]

instance SimulinkSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for detailed dynamic behavior simulation integrated with digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "UML class diagram defining concepts for data aggregation and abstraction in manufacturing contexts as per Digital Shadow Reference Model"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OntologyEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataPreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware component performing aggregation, abstraction, filtering, normalization, and feature extraction of raw sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, BehavioralAnalysisService
]

instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML toolchain enabling multi-layered system modeling across requirements, functional, logical, and technical architecture layers"
    DTDFVocab:enables MultiPerspectiveVisualizationService
]

instance MontiArcEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc framework supporting component-based architecture description and state machine modeling"
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralAnalysisService
]

instance SimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation tool integration (Simulink and other engines) for dynamic behavior modeling and analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralAnalysisService
]

instance OntologyEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Ontology-driven data management ensuring semantic interoperability between heterogeneous models and data streams"
    DTDFVocab:enables MultiPerspectiveVisualizationService
]

instance ETLIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ETL pipeline for unifying heterogeneous data sources (PLCs, SCADA systems) into standardized formats for digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time and periodic data aggregation and abstraction service for system monitoring using preprocessed sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemMonitoringInsight
]

instance BehavioralAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of state machines and simulation models to analyze system behavior dynamically"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemBehaviorInsight
]

instance MultiPerspectiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Consolidation of heterogeneous models (SysML, ADLs, simulation) into unified visualization for multi-stakeholder integration"
    DTDFVocab:provides UnifiedSystemViewInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance SystemMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational status and performance metrics derived from aggregated sensor data"
]

instance SystemBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavioral patterns and state transitions analyzed through integrated simulation and state machine models"
]

instance UnifiedSystemViewInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Consolidated multi-perspective view combining functional, logical, and technical architecture representations"
]

// C1: Environment (System Under Study)
instance IndustrialSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Complex heterogeneous industrial system modeled using MBSE methodologies with multi-layered abstraction"
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, PressureSensor, VibrationSensor,
                   PerformanceIndicatorSensor, OperationalStateSensor,
                   SysMLFunctionalModel, MontiArcAutomatonModel,
                   SimulinkSimulationModel, DigitalShadowReferenceModel
]

// C2: Components (Multiplicities)
instance DigitalTwinInstance : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Individual digital twin instance corresponding to specific physical object or subsystem in decentralized architecture"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler, SysMLModelingEnabler,
                   MontiArcEnabler, SimulationEnabler
]

instance SubDigitalTwinInstance : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Sub-twin component representing decomposed part of hierarchical digital twin structure"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance CentralOrchestrationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized coordination mechanism managing interactions between multiple digital twin instances"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ETLIntegrationEnabler, OntologyEnabler
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is orchestrated through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, leveraging **multi-notation modeling** (e.g., SysML for architecture, Simulink for behavior, or general-purpose languages like C++/Java for implementation). The system employs **architecture patterns** rooted in **component & connector perspectives** (e.g., ADLs like ACME, AADL, or MontiArc), where components represent **digital twin instances**, and connectors define their **interfaces, interconnections, and composition**. **Resource management** is implied through the integration of **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) and **heterogeneous modeling tools**, suggesting a **service-oriented or microservice-based architecture** for dynamic resource allocation. **System-wide coordination** is achieved via **behavioral models** (e.g., state machines in SysML v1.7 or MontiArc Automaton), which enable **real-time or near-real-time synchronization** between digital and physical layers. The constellation may also incorporate **active components** (as opposed to passive digital shadows) to interpret and process data, aligning with the **two-way communication** inherent in Digital Twins."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **validation and verification (V&V) methods** aligned with **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** principles, though specific fidelity requirements or uncertainty quantification techniques are not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. The system relies on **automated data flows** (e.g., from digital shadows) to ensure data integrity, with **active components** (e.g., DT-specific software) responsible for interpreting and processing incoming data streams. Error detection and correction mechanisms are implied through the use of **simulation tools (e.g., Simulink, other simulation environments)** and **modeling languages (e.g., SysML state machines, MontiArc Automaton)**, which may include **formal verification** or **runtime monitoring** to maintain model accuracy. Quality assurance processes are likely tied to **stakeholder-specific perspectives** and **heterogeneous tool integration**, ensuring traceability across abstraction layers (requirements → functional → logical → technical). However, explicit details on **uncertainty sources, error correction protocols, or quantitative fidelity metrics** are absent from the provided context."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. However, the context implies the following inferred requirements based on broader DT system design principles (though not directly stated):
  - **Communication Protocols**: Likely includes **industrial protocols (e.g., OPC UA, Modbus, Profibus)** for machine-to-machine communication, alongside **IP-based protocols (e.g., MQTT, HTTP/REST)** for cloud/edge integration.
  - **Network Topology and Architecture**: May adopt a **hybrid architecture** combining **edge computing (for low-latency local processing)** and **cloud (for centralized analytics or global coordination)**, as suggested by the need to integrate heterogeneous stakeholders and tools [31].
  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**: Critical for real-time applications; latency-sensitive operations (e.g., control commands) may require **dedicated high-speed networks (e.g., 5G, Ethernet)** or **priority-based QoS (Quality of Service) mechanisms**.
  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**: Not specified, but implied for mission-critical systems (e.g., **failover protocols, data replication, or network redundancy**).
  - **Security Protocols**: Likely includes **encryption (TLS/SSL), authentication (OAuth, certificates), and access control (RBAC)** to secure data in transit and at rest, though not explicitly mentioned in the document."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. However, the context suggests the following inferred requirements based on MBSE and DT system design principles:
  - **Hosting Platforms**: Supports **cloud (e.g., AWS IoT, Azure Digital Twins), edge (e.g., local gateways, fog nodes), or on-premise** deployments, depending on latency, security, and compliance needs.
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**: Varies by use case; may involve **high-performance computing (HPC) for simulations** (e.g., coupled with MathWorks Simulink or other simulation tools [28]–[30]) and **scalable storage (e.g., NoSQL databases for time-series data)**.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: Implied to handle **dynamic workloads** (e.g., auto-scaling in cloud environments, containerization with Docker/Kubernetes for edge deployments).
  - **Deployment Models**: Likely supports **hybrid models** (e.g., edge for real-time processing + cloud for analytics) and **modular deployment** to align with **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) that emphasize layered abstraction.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: Not specified, but critical for DT systems; may include **SLA-backed cloud services, redundant edge nodes, or disaster recovery plans**."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates horizontally with **external systems** through **interoperability standards** and **data exchange protocols**, though specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST) are not mentioned. The document emphasizes the need to **cope with models in different notations and tools**, implying **heterogeneous integration patterns** (e.g., middleware-based, API-driven, or event-based). Dependencies on external systems are inferred from the **MBSE context**, where **stakeholder perspectives** (e.g., enterprise systems, IoT platforms, or third-party simulations) must be harmonized. Integration with **other Digital Twins** or **enterprise systems** is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** and **modeling tools** (e.g., SysML IBDs for structural interoperability or Simulink for behavioral alignment). The system may adopt **federated architectures** to manage distributed data sources, ensuring **semantic consistency** across integrated components."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system, as described in the provided documents, integrates **cybersecurity protocols** and **access control mechanisms** through the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework (referenced in [46], [47], and [50]). The AAS standardizes data exchange and interoperability while embedding **authentication systems** via **OAuth 2.0** and **JWT (JSON Web Tokens)** for secure communication between components (as implied by [50], which outlines AAS as a self-describing digital entity with embedded security policies).

For **threat mitigation**, the system leverages **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** via **Mosquitto** ([52]), a lightweight publish-subscribe protocol designed for IoT and industrial environments, ensuring encrypted data transmission (TLS/SSL support). **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied in the **RAMI4.0 (Reference Architecture Model Industrie 4.0)** ([48], [49]), which mandates hierarchical security zones (e.g., *Product*, *Field*, *Enterprise*) with role-based access control (RBAC) to isolate critical assets. **Risk assessment** aligns with **IEC 62443** standards (inferred from [48]), focusing on network segmentation and anomaly detection.

**Safety-critical systems** are addressed through **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI)** ([51]), enabling tool-independent simulation model exchange with deterministic behavior validation. The **Digital Shadow** concept ([42]) further ensures real-time synchronization of physical and virtual assets, reducing operational risks via **model-driven engineering (MDE)** ([43]) with formal verification of safety constraints (e.g., collision avoidance in robotic arms like the **MABI MAX-100-2-25-P** ([44])).

**Friction modeling** for robot dynamics ([45]) integrates into the Digital Twin’s **structured learning** of physical constraints, ensuring predictive maintenance and failure prevention. The **ThingsBoard IoT platform** ([53]) provides additional **device authentication** and **data encryption** layers for edge-to-cloud communication."
]
